meta.title: Poetry Collection — Set 4
meta.subject: English Reading
meta.description: Fill-in and sentence practice for the Poetry Collection — all three poems.

questions.question: In 'Me!', the speaker says a bird can _______ from tree to tree — it moves very fast!
questions.explanation: 'Zoom' means to move very fast. The bird 'zooms' from tree to tree when it flies.

questions.question: In 'Me!', can a fish skip or run?
options: A fish can skip but not run
No — only people can skip and run
Fish can run but not skip
questions.explanation: The poem says 'But can a fish skip or run?' — the answer is no. A fish can swim but cannot skip or run. That is why the speaker is happy to be a person.

questions.question: In 'Me!', a bird can learn to read.
questions.explanation: The poem says 'But can a bird learn to read?' — the answer is no! Only people can read. That is another reason the speaker is happy to be themselves.

questions.question: In 'Sandcastle', the sand is 'soft and _______, wet and brown.' It feels very soft when wet.
questions.explanation: 'Squishy' means soft and wet, like wet sand or mud. 'Soft and squishy, wet and brown' describes how the beach sand feels.

questions.question: In 'Sandcastle', the children 'fill a bucket.' What does 'fill' mean?
options: Put things in until it is full
questions.explanation: 'Fill' means to put things inside until something is full. The children fill their bucket with sand — they put sand in until the bucket is full.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a line from 'Me!':
questions.explanation: Correct line: 'It might be fun to be a fish.' This is the opening line of the poem 'Me!' — the speaker imagines being a fish.

questions.question: In 'The Box', Dad _______ a hole into the box — he cut it carefully with a tool.
questions.hint: Cut carefully with a tool (past tense)
questions.explanation: 'Carved' is the past tense of 'carve,' which means to cut carefully. Dad 'carved a little hole' — he cut a hole in the box.

questions.question: In 'Sandcastle', the children 'gather sticks to make a gate.' This means they collect sticks.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Gather' means to collect or bring together. The children 'gather sticks' — they collect sticks from the beach to use as a gate for their castle.

questions.question: Sort these vocabulary words — are they from 'The Box' or 'Sandcastle'?
questions.explanation: 'Dragon,' 'carved,' and 'cardboard' are from 'The Box.' 'Moat,' 'squishy,' and 'decorate' are from 'Sandcastle.'

questions.question: In 'Sandcastle', there is a _______ around the castle — a ditch of water for protection.
questions.hint: A water ditch around a castle
questions.explanation: A 'moat' is a deep water ditch that goes around a castle to protect it. In the poem, 'a moat surrounds it all.'

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a line from 'Sandcastle':
questions.explanation: Wait — the actual line is 'Fill a bucket, fill a cup, / Dump it out, then build it up.' These are the instructions for building a sandcastle.

questions.question: In 'Me!', the sea is described as 'deep, blue.' Choose the word that BEST describes the sea here.
questions.explanation: 'The deep, blue sea' — 'deep' means very far below the surface, and 'blue' is its color. The sea is vast, deep, and blue.

questions.question: When the poem says 'crawled inside a cardboard cave,' it means the child literally found a real cave.
questions.explanation: No! The box is described as a 'cardboard cave' — but it is just a box. The child is using imagination to pretend the box is a cave. 'Cardboard cave' is a metaphor.

questions.question: The poem says the sandcastle will soon 'melt _______.' This means it will slowly disappear.
questions.hint: The castle disappears in this direction
questions.explanation: 'Melt away' means to slowly dissolve and disappear. The ocean waves cause the sandcastle to 'melt away' — it slowly falls apart in the water.

questions.question: Sort these words from the poems — are they NOUNS (things/places) or VERBS (actions)?
questions.explanation: 'Cave' (a hollow place), 'moat' (a water ditch), and 'dragon' (a mythical creature) are nouns. 'Scoop' (to pick up), 'crawled' (moved on hands and knees), and 'zoom' (to move fast) are verbs.
